heroHeadline: WHCD Shooter Arraigned; Manifesto Reveals Hit List — Iran Rejects Hormuz Deal on Day 59
heroSubtitle: On April 27, Cole Tomas Allen appeared in federal court for arraignment after prosecutors revealed a manifesto in which he laid out a prioritized plan to target Trump administration officials by rank — escalating the WHCD shooting from a random attack to a premeditated conspiracy. Trump held a National Security Council meeting on Iran as Tehran offered to reopen the Strait of Hormuz without nuclear concessions; the administration rejected the offer, declaring the US 'has all the cards.' The ceasefire holds on Day 59 but diplomacy is at full impasse — no second Islamabad round scheduled, Iran FM refuses to negotiate 'under shadow of threats,' and oil prices spiked on the failed offer.
footerNote: Data sourced from White House, Federal Register, executive orders, court records, Al Jazeera, CNN, Reuters, AP, NPR, Bloomberg, CNBC, Axios, NBC News, CBS News, Fox News, Gulf News, The National, Newsweek, Nate Silver/Silver Bulletin, YouGov/Economist, Fox News, BLS, Peterson Institute for International Economics, Tax Foundation, CFR, EIA, Treasury, Goldman Sachs, Federal Reserve, OECD, Cato Institute, Penn Wharton Budget Model, IMF.
